# MCP reference

## Tool: help

Print the manifest-derived list of verbs as JSON; intended for SessionStart / SessionEnd reason=compact hooks.

### Input schema

- (no input fields)

### Output schema

- `verbs`: type=[]VerbSummary ‚Äî Array of {name, description} for every verb in the manifest.

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: spawn

Launch a tracked Claude Code instance inside a new tmux session. Fire-and-forget: returns the claude_instance_id; state moves from pending to waiting on the first SessionStart hook.

### Input schema

- `cwd`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Absolute (or ~/-prefixed) path the Spawn's Claude starts in. Required.
- `template`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Optional named template under ~/.agent-director/templates/. Per-call params layer on top per SRD ¬ß7.1 (scalars replace; maps merge; permissions arrays concat; claude_args replaces wholesale).
- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Optional explicit id (UUID4 minted when absent). Collision against a live row returns ErrInstanceIdCollision.
- `label`: type=[]string (k=v), required=false ‚Äî Repeated KEY=VALUE pairs. Each becomes AGENT_DIRECTOR_LABEL_<UPPER_KEY> on the session env and persists in labels.
- `allow`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Repeated permissions.allow entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.
- `deny`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Repeated permissions.deny entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.
- `ask`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Repeated permissions.ask entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.
- `relay-mode`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî on / off. Empty falls back to config defaults.relay_mode (default off).
- `extra-env`: type=[]string (K=V), required=false ‚Äî Repeated KEY=VALUE pairs injected on the tmux session env. Reserved keys (AGENT_DIRECTOR_*) rejected; auth env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) allowed.
- `claude_args`: type=[]string (after --), required=false ‚Äî Pass-through argv to `claude` after the supervisor's own flags. Denied: --settings, --resume, --continue, --print, --output-format.
- `no-pre-trust`: type=bool, required=false ‚Äî Skip pre-writing projects.<cwd>.hasTrustDialogAccepted=true into the spawn's .claude.json (resolves to <CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR>/.claude.json if CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set in extra-env, otherwise ~/.claude.json). Default off (pre-trust IS performed so Claude Code skips its workspace-trust dialog and the Spawn becomes interactive immediately).
- `tmux-session-name`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Optional explicit tmux session name. Empty/omitted falls back to <basename(cwd)>-<id[:8]>. Validated app-side: rejects empty (when supplied), '#' ':' '.', ASCII control chars, non-UTF-8, and >64 bytes. NO DB uniqueness check; live-collision surfaces as the wrapped tmux new-session error. Name reuse across ended spawns is supported.

### Output schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string ‚Äî The id (caller-supplied or freshly-minted UUID4) the row is tracked under.

### Errors

- `ErrCwdMissing`
- `ErrCwdNotAPath`
- `ErrCwdNotFound`
- `ErrCwdNotADirectory`
- `ErrRelayModeInvalid`
- `ErrSpawnDeniedFlag`
- `ErrReservedEnvKey`
- `ErrInstanceIdCollision`
- `ErrTmuxSessionNameEmpty`
- `ErrTmuxSessionNameInvalid`
- `ErrTmuxSessionNameTooLong`
- `ErrTmuxNotAvailable`
- `ErrTmuxSessionCreate`
- `ErrTemplateNotFound`
- `ErrTemplateMalformed`
- `ErrTemplateNameUnsafe`

## Tool: status

Return the current state of a tracked Spawn (pending/waiting/working/ask_user/check_permission/ended/missing).

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn to inspect.

### Output schema

- `state`: type=string ‚Äî Current state column value.

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`

## Tool: get

Return the full DB row for a tracked Spawn (id, parent, state, cwd, session name, args, relay mode, session_id, labels, timestamps).

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn to fetch.

### Output schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string ‚Äî Stable id of the Spawn.
- `parent_id`: type=string ‚Äî Parent Spawn id (AGENT_DIRECTOR_INSTANCE_ID env at spawn time), empty when launched by a human shell.
- `state`: type=string ‚Äî Current state column value.
- `cwd`: type=string ‚Äî Canonicalized cwd.
- `tmux_session_name`: type=string ‚Äî tmux session under which the Spawn is running.
- `claude_args`: type=[]string ‚Äî Verbatim argv passed through to claude after --settings.
- `relay_mode`: type=string ‚Äî on / off.
- `jsonl_path`: type=string ‚Äî Last known transcript path. Empty until a future Epic persists it; resume composes the path on demand from cwd + claude_session_id.
- `claude_session_id`: type=string ‚Äî Claude Code session UUID, extracted from SessionStart hook's transcript_path.
- `labels`: type=map[string]string ‚Äî Caller-supplied labels.
- `started_at`: type=timestamp ‚Äî Row insert time.
- `last_seen_at`: type=timestamp ‚Äî Last hook UPSERT time.
- `ended_at`: type=timestamp? ‚Äî Set when state moves to ended (omitted while live).
- `permission_requests`: type=[]object ‚Äî All open (undecided) permission requests awaiting orchestrator decision. Always a non-null array ([] when empty). Populated only when state == check_permission; empty array for all other states. Each element: request_id (int) ‚Äî autoincrement row id; request_token (string) ‚Äî UUIDv4 token minted by runRelay, pass to decide verb to target this row; tool_name (string) ‚Äî Claude Code tool that triggered the request; tool_input (string) ‚Äî raw JSON string of the tool's input, NOT a nested object (consumers parse it themselves); requested_at (RFC3339 timestamp) ‚Äî created_at of the row.

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`

## Tool: send-keys

Send text into a tracked Spawn's tmux pane. `\r` bytes are stripped (prevent premature submission); `\n` bytes are preserved (composed-but-unsubmitted newlines in Claude's input box); a single Enter is always appended to submit the composed buffer.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the live Spawn to drive.
- `text`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Text to type into the Spawn's input. `\r` stripped pre-send; `\n` preserved as newline-in-input.
- `allow_pending`: type=bool, required=false ‚Äî When true, also permit send-keys on a pending Spawn (pre-SessionStart use case). ended/missing Spawns are still rejected even with this flag set.

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`
- `ErrSpawnNotInteractive`
- `ErrSendKeysWhileRelayed`
- `ErrTmuxNotAvailable`
- `ErrTmuxSendKeys`

## Tool: read-pane

Capture the last N lines of a tracked Spawn's tmux pane. Default 25 lines, no upper cap. Default ANSI handling strips escape codes but preserves unicode TUI glyphs (‚ùØ, ‚éø, üêù). `ansi=true` returns raw bytes.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn to read.
- `n_lines`: type=int, required=false ‚Äî Number of trailing pane lines to return. Defaults to 25 when 0/omitted. No upper cap.
- `ansi`: type=bool, required=false ‚Äî When true, return raw bytes from tmux (escape codes preserved). When false (default), strip ANSI sequences while preserving unicode glyphs.
- `allow_pending`: type=bool, required=false ‚Äî Accepted for surface symmetry with send-keys. ReadPane has no state guard (pending/ended/missing are all readable), so this flag has no behavioral effect.

### Output schema

- `pane`: type=string ‚Äî Captured pane text. ANSI handling depends on the `ansi` parameter.

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`
- `ErrTmuxNotAvailable`
- `ErrTmuxCaptureFailed`

## Tool: kill

Terminate the Spawn's tmux session. Idempotent on terminal states (ended/missing). Does NOT promise state cleanup ‚Äî the row stays in its prior state until find-missing reconciles it.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn to kill.

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`

## Tool: decide

Orchestrator's allow/deny verdict on an open PermissionRequest. Race-free first-call-wins via a single-statement UPDATE guarded by `decision IS NULL`. Only callable on Spawns with relay_mode=on.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn sitting on the PermissionRequest.
- `request_token`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî UUIDv4 token identifying the specific permission request to decide. Minted by runRelay per-request; required to enforce per-row isolation when multiple concurrent requests exist for the same Spawn.
- `decision`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Either `allow` or `deny`.
- `reason`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Currently discarded on deny; the canonical DecisionReasonOperator is persisted regardless. Reserved for future schema additions.

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- `ErrMissingRequestToken`
- `ErrSpawnNotFound`
- `ErrRelayModeOff`
- `ErrNoOpenPermissionRequest`
- `ErrAlreadyDecided`
- `ErrAmbiguousRequest`
- `ErrInvalidDecision`

## Tool: get-permission

Fetch a single permission_requests row by request_token. Token-only lookup (SR-3.5: UUIDv4 is globally selective); no claude_instance_id required. Nullable columns (decision, decision_reason, decided_at) surface as JSON null while the row is open.

### Input schema

- `request_token`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî UUIDv4 token identifying the permission_requests row to fetch.

### Output schema

- `request_token`: type=string ‚Äî UUIDv4 token the row is keyed under (echoed back).
- `request_id`: type=int ‚Äî Autoincrement primary key of the permission_requests row.
- `tool_name`: type=string ‚Äî Claude Code tool that triggered the permission request (e.g. "Bash", "Write").
- `tool_input`: type=string ‚Äî Raw JSON string of the tool's input as stored in the DB; NOT a nested JSON object. Passes through byte-identical from the DB column ‚Äî consumers parse it themselves.
- `requested_at`: type=timestamp ‚Äî RFC3339 timestamp when the row was created (maps from the created_at DB column).
- `decision`: type=string? ‚Äî "allow" or "deny" once decided; null while the row is open (decision IS NULL in the DB).
- `decision_reason`: type=string? ‚Äî Canonical decision-reason string for deny rows (operator / timeout / find_missing per SR-1.3); null for open rows AND for allow rows (closed-allow carries no reason).
- `decided_at`: type=timestamp? ‚Äî RFC3339 timestamp when the verdict was written; null while the row is open.

### Errors

- `ErrPermissionRequestNotFound`

## Tool: resume

Bring a terminated (ended/missing) Spawn back to life via `claude --resume`. Same claude_instance_id, fresh tmux session, same JSONL transcript. parent_id is re-derived from the caller's AGENT_DIRECTOR_INSTANCE_ID env var on every resume.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the terminated Spawn to resurrect.

### Output schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string ‚Äî The same id passed in (resume preserves the instance id across resurrection).

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`
- `ErrSpawnNotResumable`
- `ErrNoSessionId`
- `ErrJsonlMissing`
- `ErrTmuxNotAvailable`
- `ErrTmuxSessionCreate`

## Tool: find-missing

Reconcile DB state against live processes. Scans live-state rows (including pending), diffs against the OS probe (Linux /proc / macOS sysctl), transitions unprobeable rows to `missing`. Degraded-mode guard: 0 readable processes + ‚â•1 live rows ‚Üí log warning + refuse to write.

### Input schema

- (no input fields)

### Output schema

- `count`: type=int ‚Äî Number of rows transitioned to missing on this sweep. Zero is a legitimate happy-path result when nothing needed reaping (or when the degraded-mode guard refused to write).
- `ids`: type=[]string ‚Äî Sorted IDs of rows transitioned to missing.

### Errors

- `ErrProbeUnsupported`

## Tool: expire

Remove terminal-state rows (ended/missing) whose ended_at is older than the retention window. Default window is config defaults.expire_retention_days; --older-than overrides. Does NOT touch tmux or JSONL transcripts.

### Input schema

- `older_than`: type=duration, required=false ‚Äî Duration override (e.g. `7d`, `2h`, `0d`). When omitted, defaults.expire_retention_days from config applies.

### Output schema

- `count`: type=int ‚Äî Number of rows removed. Zero is a legitimate happy-path result when no terminal rows matched the retention window.
- `ids`: type=[]string ‚Äî Sorted IDs of rows removed.

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: delete

Admin batch removal by claude_instance_id. Bypasses all guards. Does NOT touch tmux sessions or JSONL transcripts. Per-row result map records ok/error per id; the batch never aborts on a partial failure.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=[]string, required=true ‚Äî Id(s) to delete. Repeatable on CLI; JSON array via MCP.

### Output schema

- `results`: type=map[string]string ‚Äî Per-id result: "ok" on success, an err_name string on failure.

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: make-template

Save a reusable spawn preset. The TOML file lands under ~/.agent-director/templates/<name>.toml; spawn --template <name> applies it. Reserved per-invocation params (template, claude_instance_id, tmux_session_name) are NOT accepted.

### Input schema

- `name`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Template name. Must be filename-safe (no path separators, no leading dot, no `..`).
- `cwd`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Bake a default cwd into the template. Per-call --cwd overrides.
- `relay_mode`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Bake a default relay_mode (on/off). Per-call --relay-mode overrides.
- `claude_args`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake default Claude argv. Per-call --claude-args REPLACES the template's array wholesale (not concat).
- `extra_env`: type=map[string]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake env-var entries. Per-call --extra-env merges by key; per-call wins on collision.
- `label`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake label k=v entries. Per-call --label merges by key; per-call wins on collision.
- `allow`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake permissions.allow entries. Per-call --allow CONCATENATES (does not replace).
- `deny`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake permissions.deny entries. Per-call --deny CONCATENATES.
- `ask`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Bake permissions.ask entries. Per-call --ask CONCATENATES.
- `overwrite`: type=bool, required=false ‚Äî Replace any existing template at this name atomically. Default false preserves O_EXCL create-only semantics.

### Output schema

- `path`: type=string ‚Äî Absolute path of the written template file.

### Errors

- `ErrTemplateNameUnsafe`
- `ErrTemplateExists`
- `ErrTemplateMalformed`

## Tool: list

Enumerate Spawn rows. All filters AND together. Returned order is unspecified ‚Äî callers sort with jq etc.

### Input schema

- `state`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Filter by state. Multiple values OR together. Comma-separated on CLI; JSON array via MCP.
- `label`: type=[]string, required=false ‚Äî Filter by label k=v. Repeatable on CLI; each entry must contain a literal `=`. Multiple entries AND together.
- `parent`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Filter by parent_id exact match.
- `cwd`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Filter by canonicalized cwd exact match.
- `tmux-session-name`: type=string, required=false ‚Äî Filter by tmux session name exact match. Returns any live or ended row whose tmux_session_name equals the value byte-for-byte; correlation across re-uses, not uniqueness enforcement.
- `limit`: type=int, required=false ‚Äî Cap result count. 0 / omitted means no cap.

### Output schema

- `spawns`: type=[]Spawn ‚Äî Matching rows. Empty array when none match (never null).

### Errors

- `ErrListInvalidLabel`

## Tool: pause

Politely shut down a waiting Spawn by sending `/exit` and waiting up to pause.timeout_seconds for the row to reach `ended`. One-shot ‚Äî no caller-side polling. Terminal states (ended/missing) are no-op success.

### Input schema

- `claude_instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Id of the Spawn to pause.

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- `ErrSpawnNotFound`
- `ErrSpawnNotPausable`
- `ErrPauseTimeout`
- `ErrTmuxNotAvailable`
- `ErrTmuxSendKeys`

## Tool: serve

Start the stdio MCP server. Long-lived process that exposes every other verb as an MCP tool over JSON-RPC on stdin/stdout. Typically registered with `claude mcp add agent-director <binary-path> serve --stdio`.

### Input schema

- `stdio`: type=bool, required=true ‚Äî Enter the stdio MCP loop (required for v1; other transports may land in future Epics).

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: version

Print the binary's build-time version stamp as JSON ({version, commit}). Used by install.sh to verify a local binary matches the current source tree before installing it.

### Input schema

- (no input fields)

### Output schema

- `version`: type=string ‚Äî Human-readable version stamp from `git describe --tags --always --dirty` at build time. "dev" for unstamped builds.
- `commit`: type=string ‚Äî Full git SHA the binary was built from. "unknown" for unstamped builds.

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: trail-emit

Emit an ad.* audit-trail event directly. Sub-verb: relay-attempt. Does not open state.db ‚Äî works in corrupted-state recovery scenarios (SR-A-2.3).

### Input schema

- `sub_verb`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî Sub-verb to invoke. Currently: relay-attempt.
- `token`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî request_token. Required.
- `endpoint`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî target_endpoint (URL or socket path). Required.
- `outcome`: type=string|int, required=true ‚Äî 3-digit HTTP status code (100-599, emitted as integer) or named error class: connection_refused, timeout, dns_failure (emitted as string). Required.
- `bytes_sent`: type=int, required=false ‚Äî Bytes sent. Default 0.
- `bytes_received`: type=int, required=false ‚Äî Bytes received. Default 0.
- `instance_id`: type=string, required=true ‚Äî claude_instance_id. Required.

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- `ErrInvalidFlags`
- `ErrTrailWrite`

## Tool: trail-path

Print the resolved trail file path (one line, no JSON envelope) and exit 0. Path is taken from $AGENT_DIRECTOR_STATE_DIR when set, otherwise ~/.agent-director/ad-trail.jsonl. Does not open state.db ‚Äî works in corrupted-state recovery scenarios (SR-A-6). Output is a raw path + newline, not a JSON envelope, so shell callers can use `cd $(agent-director trail-path | xargs dirname)` directly.

### Input schema

- (no input fields)

### Output schema

- `path`: type=string ‚Äî Absolute path of the trail file (printed to stdout as a raw string, not a JSON field).

### Errors

- (none)

## Tool: hook

Internal: invoked by Claude Code on lifecycle events via the per-Spawn --settings hooks. Reads payload JSON from stdin, writes a row UPSERT, exits 0 (state-tracking fail-open).

### Input schema

- `stdin`: type=json, required=true ‚Äî Claude Code hook payload (hook_event_name, transcript_path, tool_name, reason, ...).

### Output schema

- (no output fields)

### Errors

- (none)
